name: Roasted Spare Ribs (Pei Quot)
recipeInstructions:
Remove as much fat and gristle from the meat and cut into individual ribs. Wash black beans and mash them. Mince ginger root and scallion.
Smash, peel, and mince garlic. Mix mashed black beans, minced ginger root, and minced garlic.
Mix soy sauce, honey, hoisin sauce, and rice wine.
COOKING:
Heat oil in a large deep skillet or wok. Add ginger root and scallion. Stir-fry for 1 minute. Add ribs and stir-fry for 5 minutes. Add the soy sauce mixture; stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add chicken broth. Stir ribs to coat with sauce and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring ribs every 5 or 6 minutes to coat with sauce.
Remove ribs from skillet and spread in a large shallow roasting pan, in one layer if possible. Roast at 350°F for 10 minutes. If ribs are not dry after 10 minutes, roast a little longer. They may also be barbecue grilled on an outdoor grill instead of roasted in the oven.
NOTE: This sauce is quite spicy. For a milder dish, omit hoisin sauce.
SOURCE: ORIENTAL COOKING by Schryver
